pattern: Greedy interval scheduling: sort by right endpoint and greedily shoot each arrow at the farthest-right point covered by the current cluster of overlapping balloons.
hints: If you sort balloons by their right boundary, you can decide where to shoot an arrow without regretting the choice later.
After sorting by right endpoint, track where the current arrow is aimed. Any balloon whose left boundary exceeds that position requires a new arrow.

Sort by xend. Initialize the arrow position at points[0][1]. For each subsequent balloon, if its xstart > arrow position, increment the count and update the arrow position to the current balloon's xend.

edge_inputs.description: four overlapping balloons needing two arrows
no overlaps, each balloon needs its own arrow
touching at single endpoint still counts as overlap
single balloon requires exactly one arrow
all balloons fully overlapping at one point
two separate groups with no overlap between groups

stress.description: large random set of intervals over a wide coordinate range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    points = []
    for _ in range(size):
        a = rng.randint(-10**9, 10**9 - 1)
        b = rng.randint(a + 1, min(a + 10**6, 10**9))
        points.append([a, b])
    return [points]
stress.note: intervals of varying widths scattered across full range
